hypotheses.hypothesis: Among self-secreting cytokine-armored CAR-T designs targeting GPC3 in hepatocellular carcinoma, IL-15 will demonstrate superior efficacy in reducing tumor burden and enhancing CAR-T cell persistence compared to IL-2, IL-7, IL-12, and IL-18.

candidate_intervention_arms.expected_mechanism: IL-2 promotes T cell proliferation and activation but may lead to exhaustion in a solid tumor environment.
supporting_evidence: Gene-Edited Interleukin CAR-T Cells Therapy in the Treatment of Malignancies: Present and Future (2021)
Bispecific LV20.19 CAR T-Cells Expanded in IL-7 and IL-15 Have Greater Polyfunctionality and Polyfunctional Strength Than CAR T-Cells Expanded in IL-2 (2021)

candidate_intervention_arms.expected_mechanism: IL-7 enhances T cell survival and memory formation, potentially improving persistence in the tumor microenvironment.
supporting_evidence: Updated results from first-in-human phase 1 dose-escalation trial of TAK-102, a GPC3-targeted armored CAR T cells (2024)
GPC3-IL7-CCL19-CAR-T primes immune microenvironment reconstitution for hepatocellular carcinoma therapy (2023)

candidate_intervention_arms.expected_mechanism: IL-12 enhances T cell activation and promotes a Th1 response, potentially improving anti-tumor efficacy.
supporting_evidence: Hypoxia-Inducible IL-12 and IL-23 Co-Armored CAR T Cells Provide Safety and Enhanced Efficacy in Preclinical Models of Neuroblastoma (2026)
Antigen-dependent IL-12 signaling in CAR T cells promotes regional to systemic disease targeting (2023)

candidate_intervention_arms.expected_mechanism: IL-15 promotes T cell survival and enhances memory T cell formation, leading to improved persistence and anti-tumor activity.
supporting_evidence: Glypican-3–Specific CAR T Cells Coexpressing IL15 and IL21 Have Superior Expansion and Antitumor Activity against Hepatocellular Carcinoma (2020)
IL-15 receptor alpha reduces the toxicity of IL-15 during CAR-T immunotherapy (2022)

candidate_intervention_arms.expected_mechanism: IL-18 enhances T cell activation and promotes a pro-inflammatory environment, potentially improving anti-tumor responses.
supporting_evidence: Single-day nonactivated IL-18-armed CAR T cells establish a durable, stemlike state with enhanced persistence (2026)
IL-18–secreting CAR T cells targeting DLL3 are highly effective in small cell lung cancer models (2023)

fixed_scenario_constraints: low tumor/cancer cell surface antigen density
